94.reason: The loop condition relies on 'node' (total vertices) but doesn't correctly handle the BFS termination or queue emptiness. It assumes exactly node-1 more items will be processed, which might fail if the graph structure changes or if the queue empties early (though problem says connected). More critically, it uses a fixed count instead of checking if the queue is empty (front <= rear).

It assumes exactly node-1 more items will be processed, which might fail if the graph structure changes or if the queue empties early (though problem says connected). More critically, it uses a fixed count instead of checking if the queue is empty (front <= rear).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Logic Error: The loop condition and logic are flawed. It relies on 'node' count decrementing correctly only when unvisited nodes are found, but fails to handle cases where neighbors are already visited or queue empties prematurely. Also, it doesn't strictly follow BFS layer-by-layer if not all nodes are reachable in the expected count steps due to previous visits.]
